name: Topological sort (Kahn's algorithm)
task: Create /tmp/eval_topo/topo.py with a function toposort(graph) that returns a topological ordering of the nodes, or None if the graph contains a cycle. Input format: graph is a dict where graph[node] is the list of PREREQUISITES for that node (i.e., they must appear earlier in the output). Every node referenced anywhere must appear in the output. Use Kahn's algorithm (BFS on indegree).

validation.script: import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_topo/topo.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); ts = mod.toposort; g = {'shoes':['socks'],'socks':[],'shirt':[],'pants':[],'jacket':['shirt','pants']}; o = ts(g); assert o is not None; pos = {n:i for i,n in enumerate(o)}; assert pos['socks'] < pos['shoes']; assert pos['shirt'] < pos['jacket']; assert pos['pants'] < pos['jacket']; assert ts({'a':['b'],'b':['a']}) is None; print('OK')
